name: Brian Jones Death
description: The death of Brian Jones, the founding member and original leader of The Rolling Stones, on July 3, 1969, remains a subject of intense scrutiny and speculation. Officially ruled as 'death by misadventure' due to drowning in his swimming pool after consuming alcohol and drugs, the circumstances surrounding his demise have fueled numerous conspiracy theories. These theories typically allege that Jones was murdered, with various suspects ranging from builders working on his property to members of his own band. Proponents of foul play point to inconsistencies in witness statements, the lack of a thorough police investigation at the time, and rumors of financial disputes. Mainstream perspectives, based on police reports and coroner's findings, maintain the accidental drowning narrative, attributing it to Jones's unstable mental state and drug use. However, lingering questions and persistent rumors have kept the conspiracy theories alive, making it one of the most debated deaths in rock and roll history. The lack of definitive evidence and the passage of time make it difficult to definitively prove or disprove either the accidental death or murder scenarios.
subtitle: Rock Legend's Death: Accident or Murder?

claims.claim: Brian Jones was murdered by Frank Thorogood.
claims.evidence: Thorogood allegedly confessed to the murder on his deathbed; varying accounts of the night's events; alleged financial disputes between Jones and Thorogood.
claims.criticism: Thorogood's alleged confession is unverified and based on hearsay; inconsistencies in witness statements can be attributed to intoxication and stress; financial disputes, while present, don't definitively prove motive for murder.

claims.claim: The Rolling Stones were involved in Jones's death.
claims.evidence: Rumors of Jones being forced out of the band due to his drug use and erratic behavior; alleged power struggles within the band; speculation that the band wanted to control the narrative surrounding his death.
claims.criticism: No direct evidence links the band to Jones's death; Jones's expulsion from the band was a business decision due to his unreliability; controlling the narrative doesn't equate to involvement in a murder.

claims.claim: The police investigation was inadequate and potentially covered up evidence.
claims.evidence: Lack of a thorough forensic examination of the crime scene; allegedly biased investigation due to Jones's drug use; rumors of police corruption.
claims.criticism: While the investigation may not meet modern standards, it doesn't necessarily indicate a cover-up; police attributed the death to accidental drowning based on available evidence; rumors of corruption are unsubstantiated.

keyFigures.name: Anna Wohlin
keyFigures.role: Jones's girlfriend and witness to his death

keyFigures.name: Terry Rawlings
keyFigures.role: Author of "Who Killed Brian Jones?" proposing murder theories

keyFigures.name: David Starkey
keyFigures.role: Historian who supports the accidental death theory

timeline.event: Brian Jones is fired from The Rolling Stones.
Brian Jones is found dead in his swimming pool.
The coroner rules Jones's death as 'death by misadventure'.
Numerous books and documentaries explore alternative theories about Jones's death.

sources.title: Geoffrey Giuliano

sources.title: David Starkey
